blocks.content: Daisy was still up when her phone lit with Ryan's goodnight text. She answered it. Set the phone down.

She opened MySpace because she did most nights before bed — nothing intentional, just the habit of it, the ritual of seeing who had changed what. She went down the left panel. Eli's profile. Savannah's. She got to Kit's.

She didn't click play. She just sat there reading the title in the small font beside the player. Molly. She read it again. The house was quiet — parents asleep, no sound from the street. Just the screen and that word and the particular stillness of a late-May night in Millhaven with the window cracked and the summer already pressing in.
She looked at his top eight. Eli in the first slot. Her name second. Same as always, same as it had been since he made the profile.
